message:
fix(db/mail_explorer): tolerate orphaned project and recipient rows in inbound/outbound queries

The mail explorer's inbound and outbound SELECTs previously used
`JOIN projects p` and assumed every joined `agent` / `project` row
would have a non-empty name/slug. If a message pointed at a project
that had been deleted (orphaned `project_id`), or at a recipient whose
`agents.name` was empty/whitespace, the message was either:

* silently dropped from the result set (because the inner join on
  `projects` filtered it out), or
* rendered with a blank `project_slug` / `to_agents` cell that made the
  TUI think the row was broken.

This change makes the explorer always return the rows even when the
referenced dimension data is missing, tagged with an explicit sentinel
so operators can see which row is orphaned.

SQL changes in `fetch_inbound` and `fetch_outbound`
---------------------------------------------------
* `JOIN projects p ON p.id = m.project_id` → `LEFT JOIN`, so rows with
  orphaned `project_id` still appear.
* `p.slug AS project_slug` →
  `COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(p.slug), ''), '[unknown-project-' || m.project_id || ']') AS project_slug`.
  Missing, empty, or whitespace-only slugs are replaced with a
  sentinel that embeds the row's numeric project id so the orphan is
  identifiable.
* `GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT a_recip.name)` →
  `GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a_recip.name), ''), '[unknown-agent-' || mr2.agent_id || ']'))`
  (and the outbound variant with `mr` instead of `mr2`). Recipients
  with blank names are surfaced with the same sentinel shape, and the
  explorer no longer silently deduplicates them under a single empty
  string.

Test coverage (`tests/mail_explorer.rs`)
----------------------------------------
* New `explorer_inbound_keeps_orphaned_project_rows_visible` test:
  sets up a project and a message, then deletes the project row out
  from under the message, and asserts that the inbound query still
  returns the message with its `project_slug` matching the
  `[unknown-project-<id>]` sentinel shape. Mirrors the existing
  `explorer_inbound_keeps_orphaned_sender_rows_visible` coverage on
  the recipient side.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -474,12 +474,13 @@ async fn fetch_inbound(
             "SELECT m.id, m.project_id, m.sender_id, m.thread_id, m.subject, m.body_md, \
              m.importance, m.ack_required, m.created_ts, \
              r.kind, r.read_ts, r.ack_ts, \
-             COALESCE(s.name, '{UNKNOWN_SENDER_DISPLAY}') AS sender_name, p.slug AS project_slug, \
-             COALESCE(GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT a_recip.name), '') AS to_agents \
+             COALESCE(s.name, '{UNKNOWN_SENDER_DISPLAY}') AS sender_name, \
+             COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(p.slug), ''), '[unknown-project-' || m.project_id || ']') AS project_slug, \
+             COALESCE(GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a_recip.name), ''), '[unknown-agent-' || mr2.agent_id || ']')), '') AS to_agents \
              FROM message_recipients r \
              JOIN messages m ON m.id = r.message_id \
              LEFT JOIN agents s ON s.id = m.sender_id \
-             JOIN projects p ON p.id = m.project_id \
+             LEFT JOIN projects p ON p.id = m.project_id \
              LEFT JOIN message_recipients mr2 ON mr2.message_id = m.id \
              LEFT JOIN agents a_recip ON a_recip.id = mr2.agent_id \
              WHERE {where_clause} \
@@ -543,11 +544,12 @@ async fn fetch_outbound(
         let sql = format!(
             "SELECT m.id, m.project_id, m.sender_id, m.thread_id, m.subject, m.body_md, \
              m.importance, m.ack_required, m.created_ts, \
-             COALESCE(s.name, '{UNKNOWN_SENDER_DISPLAY}') AS sender_name, p.slug AS project_slug, \
-             COALESCE(GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT a_recip.name), '') AS to_agents \
+             COALESCE(s.name, '{UNKNOWN_SENDER_DISPLAY}') AS sender_name, \
+             COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(p.slug), ''), '[unknown-project-' || m.project_id || ']') AS project_slug, \
+             COALESCE(GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a_recip.name), ''), '[unknown-agent-' || mr.agent_id || ']')), '') AS to_agents \
              FROM messages m \
              LEFT JOIN agents s ON s.id = m.sender_id \
-             JOIN projects p ON p.id = m.project_id \
+             LEFT JOIN projects p ON p.id = m.project_id \
              LEFT JOIN message_recipients mr ON mr.message_id = m.id \
              LEFT JOIN agents a_recip ON a_recip.id = mr.agent_id \
              WHERE {where_clause} \

@@ -496,6 +496,263 @@ fn explorer_inbound_keeps_orphaned_sender_rows_visible() {
     assert_ne!(sender_id, recipient_id);
 }
 
+#[test]
+fn explorer_inbound_keeps_orphaned_project_rows_visible() {
+    let (pool, _dir) = make_pool();
+
+    let (project_id, recipient_id) = {
+        let p = pool.clone();
+        block_on(move |cx| async move {
+            let project = match queries::ensure_project(
+                &cx,
+                &p,
+                "/tmp/__mcp-agent-mail-db-test-mail-explorer-orphaned-project",
+            )
+            .await
+            {
+                Outcome::Ok(r) => r,
+                other => panic!("ensure_project failed: {other:?}"),
+            };
+            let project_id = project.id.expect("project id");
+
+            let sender = match queries::register_agent(
+                &cx,
+                &p,
+                project_id,
+                "GreenStone",
+                "codex-cli",
+                "gpt-5",
+                None,
+                None,
+                None,
+            )
+            .await
+            {
+                Outcome::Ok(r) => r,
+                other => panic!("register sender failed: {other:?}"),
+            };
+            let recipient = match queries::register_agent(
+                &cx,
+                &p,
+                project_id,
+                "BlueLake",
+                "codex-cli",
+                "gpt-5",
+                None,
+                None,
+                None,
+            )
+            .await
+            {
+                Outcome::Ok(r) => r,
+                other => panic!("register recipient failed: {other:?}"),
+            };
+
+            match queries::create_message_with_recipients(
+                &cx,
+                &p,
+                project_id,
+                sender.id.expect("sender id"),
+                "Explorer survives project drift",
+                "Body",
+                Some("explorer-orphaned-project-thread"),
+                "normal",
+                false,
+                "[]",
+                &[(recipient.id.expect("recipient id"), "to")],
+            )
+            .await
+            {
+                Outcome::Ok(_) => {}
+                other => panic!("create message failed: {other:?}"),
+            }
+
+            let conn = match p.acquire(&cx).await {
+                Outcome::Ok(conn) => conn,
+                Outcome::Err(error) => panic!("acquire failed: {error}"),
+                Outcome::Cancelled(_) => panic!("acquire cancelled"),
+                Outcome::Panicked(_) => panic!("acquire panicked"),
+            };
+            conn.execute_sync(
+                "DELETE FROM projects WHERE id = ?",
+                &[Value::BigInt(project_id)],
+            )
+            .expect("delete project row");
+            drop(conn);
+
+            (project_id, recipient.id.expect("recipient id"))
+        })
+    };
+
+    let page = block_on(move |cx| async move {
+        match fetch_explorer_page(
+            &cx,
+            &pool,
+            &ExplorerQuery {
+                agent_name: "BlueLake".into(),
+                project_id: Some(project_id),
+                direction: Direction::Inbound,
+                limit: 50,
+                ..Default::default()
+            },
+        )
+        .await
+        {
+            Outcome::Ok(v) => v,
+            other => panic!("explorer inbound failed: {other:?}"),
+        }
+    });
+
+    let entry = page
+        .entries
+        .iter()
+        .find(|entry| entry.subject == "Explorer survives project drift")
+        .expect("find orphaned-project explorer row");
+    assert_eq!(entry.project_id, project_id);
+    assert_eq!(
+        entry.project_slug,
+        format!("[unknown-project-{project_id}]")
+    );
+    assert_eq!(entry.direction, Direction::Inbound);
+    assert_eq!(page.total_count, 1);
+    assert_eq!(page.stats.inbound_count, 1);
+    assert!(page.entries.iter().any(|row| row.message_id > 0));
+    assert!(recipient_id > 0);
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn explorer_outbound_keeps_orphaned_recipient_rows_visible() {
+    let (pool, _dir) = make_pool();
+
+    let (project_id, sender_id, recipient_id) = {
+        let p = pool.clone();
+        block_on(move |cx| async move {
+            let project = match queries::ensure_project(
+                &cx,
+                &p,
+                "/tmp/__mcp-agent-mail-db-test-mail-explorer-orphaned-recipient",
+            )
+            .await
+            {
+                Outcome::Ok(r) => r,
+                other => panic!("ensure_project failed: {other:?}"),
+            };
+            let project_id = project.id.expect("project id");
+
+            let sender = match queries::register_agent(
+                &cx,
+                &p,
+                project_id,
+                "RedFox",
+                "codex-cli",
+                "gpt-5",
+                None,
+                None,
+                None,
+            )
+            .await
+            {
+                Outcome::Ok(r) => r,
+                other => panic!("register sender failed: {other:?}"),
+            };
+            let recipient = match queries::register_agent(
+                &cx,
+                &p,
+                project_id,
+                "BlueLake",
+                "codex-cli",
+                "gpt-5",
+                None,
+                None,
+                None,
+            )
+            .await
+            {
+                Outcome::Ok(r) => r,
+                other => panic!("register recipient failed: {other:?}"),
+            };
+
+            match queries::create_message_with_recipients(
+                &cx,
+                &p,
+                project_id,
+                sender.id.expect("sender id"),
+                "Explorer keeps orphaned recipients visible",
+                "Body",
+                Some("explorer-orphaned-recipient-thread"),
+                "normal",
+                false,
+                "[]",
+                &[(recipient.id.expect("recipient id"), "to")],
+            )
+            .await
+            {
+                Outcome::Ok(_) => {}
+                other => panic!("create message failed: {other:?}"),
+            }
+
+            let conn = match p.acquire(&cx).await {
+                Outcome::Ok(conn) => conn,
+                Outcome::Err(error) => panic!("acquire failed: {error}"),
+                Outcome::Cancelled(_) => panic!("acquire cancelled"),
+                Outcome::Panicked(_) => panic!("acquire panicked"),
+            };
+            conn.execute_sync(
+                "DELETE FROM agents WHERE id = ? AND project_id = ?",
+                &[
+                    Value::BigInt(recipient.id.expect("recipient id")),
+                    Value::BigInt(project_id),
+                ],
+            )
+            .expect("delete recipient row");
+            drop(conn);
+
+            (
+                project_id,
+                sender.id.expect("sender id"),
+                recipient.id.expect("recipient id"),
+            )
+        })
+    };
+
+    let page = block_on(move |cx| async move {
+        match fetch_explorer_page(
+            &cx,
+            &pool,
+            &ExplorerQuery {
+                agent_name: "RedFox".into(),
+                project_id: Some(project_id),
+                direction: Direction::Outbound,
+                limit: 50,
+                ..Default::default()
+            },
+        )
+        .await
+        {
+            Outcome::Ok(v) => v,
+            other => panic!("explorer outbound failed: {other:?}"),
+        }
+    });
+
+    let entry = page
+        .entries
+        .iter()
+        .find(|entry| entry.subject == "Explorer keeps orphaned recipients visible")
+        .expect("find orphaned-recipient explorer row");
+    assert_eq!(entry.project_id, project_id);
+    assert_eq!(entry.sender_name, "RedFox");
+    assert!(
+        entry
+            .to_agents
+            .contains(&format!("[unknown-agent-{recipient_id}]")),
+        "orphaned recipient placeholder missing from {}",
+        entry.to_agents
+    );
+    assert_eq!(page.total_count, 1);
+    assert_eq!(page.stats.outbound_count, 1);
+    assert!(sender_id > 0);
+}
+
 #[test]
 fn explorer_outbound_only() {
     let TestCorpus { pool: p, _dir, .. } = seed_corpus("outbound_only");
